TextView.android:text:
1. Purpose of the App
The Event Lottery System helps people register for popular community events in a fair way. Instead of spots being taken by whoever clicks first, users can join a waiting list and are selected randomly.
This system gives everyone a fair chance to participate, even if they cannot register immediately when an event opens.
2. General Rules
To keep the platform fair and enjoyable for everyone, please follow these rules:
- Each user may join the waiting list once per event. - Users should provide accurate personal information when registering. - If selected for an event, users must accept or decline the invitation within the given time. - If there is no response, the spot may be offered to another entrant.
3. How the Lottery Works
- Users join the waiting list during the event registration period. - When registration closes, the system randomly selects participants. - Selected users receive a notification inviting them to register. - If someone declines or does not respond, another person from the waiting list may be selected. - This process continues until all available spots are filled.
4. Responsibilities of Entrants
- Join waiting lists only for events they plan to attend. - Respond to invitations as soon as possible. - Follow any rules set by the event organizer. - Keep their profile information up to date. - Entrants can leave a waiting list at any time before the lottery draw.
5. Responsibilities of Organizers
- Provide clear and accurate event details (date, time, location, capacity). - Set registration start and end dates. - Use the lottery system fairly. - Communicate updates to entrants through the app. - Organizers may update or cancel events if necessary.
6. QR Code Access
Events may include a QR code to make registration easier. Scanning a QR code takes users directly to the event page where they can view details and join the waiting list. QR codes should only be used for legitimate event promotion.
7. Location Verification
Some events may require location verification. If enabled, the app may check device location when users join the waiting list. Location data is used only for this purpose.
8. Notifications
The app may send notifications when: - A user is selected for an event - A user is not selected - An event is updated or cancelled - Organizers send important announcements
Users can choose to disable notifications in settings.
9. Privacy and Data
Personal information is stored securely using Firebase and only used for event registration and communication. Users can delete their profile at any time.
10. Administrative Oversight
Administrators help manage the platform by removing inappropriate events, removing abusive profiles, managing uploaded images, and reviewing notification logs.
11. Fair Use
Users who misuse the platform may be removed. Examples include creating multiple accounts to increase lottery chances, uploading inappropriate/offensive content, or violating platform guidelines.
